sourceMovie.title: Django Unchained
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Django Unchained works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and western blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Hateful Eight
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Django Unchained clicked for you, The Hateful Eight (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and western, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Django Unchained?
faq.answer: Try The Hateful Eight, True Grit, Dances with Wolves—each shares DNA with Django Unchained while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Django Unchained?
faq.answer: Django Unchained (2012) is best described as Drama, Western. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Django Unchained worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 8.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
